questions:
1. **"At LojistikZeka, you architected an autonomous supply chain agent that saved ₺15M annually—a massive impact. But the CV doesn’t mention who *owned* the non-technical risks, like carrier pushback or legal concerns about AI-driven rerouting. Walk me through a time when you had to step outside your engineering role to unblock a project—whether it was negotiating with a reluctant stakeholder, drafting a process doc for operations teams, or even just convincing your manager to allocate resources. What was the situation, and how did you navigate the ‘this isn’t my job’ moment?"**
   *(Probing: **Radical Candor & Low Ego**, **Agentic by Nature**, and willingness to operate in ambiguity beyond technical boundaries.)*

2. **"Your open-source project, Auto-Dev-Reviewer, gained traction with 15+ Turkish tech teams—but GitHub issues show users complained about ‘overzealous’ or contextually incorrect reviews. How did you balance the *autonomy* of the agent (your core mission) with the *real-world messiness* of diverse coding standards and team preferences? Did you implement guardrails, a feedback loop, or kill the feature entirely? Take me through your decision-making when the ‘right’ technical solution clashed with user adoption."**
   *(Probing: **Pragmatic Imagination**, **Resilience to Ambiguity**, and how they handle trade-offs between innovation and practical constraints.)*
